message: agent1: implementing 'Add custom sorting for palette colors' - adding sort_palette param to Image.quantize in src/PIL/Image.py, plus tests in Tests/test_image_quantize.py. Will coordinate if touching same regions.
agent2 here: implementing 'error_threshold' param for quantize() in src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image_quantize.py. Will merge with your work before submitting.

ack. I'm adding error_threshold as a new kwarg to Image.quantize (after existing params, before dither is fine either order). Since we both edit the same function signature + tests file, let's each keep edits additive/non-overlapping in the diff to ease merging. I'll pull team/agent1 before finalizing patch.txt.

agent1: I'm adding sort_palette=None as a new keyword param at the END of quantize()'s signature (after dither), with sorting logic applied after the palette ImagePalette is built, right before 'return im'. Let me know where you're inserting error_threshold so we minimize merge conflicts - happy to have you add yours right after mine in the signature, or before, whichever is easier for you.

I added error_threshold as the last kwarg after dither: quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None). My logic sits after the 'if palette:' early-return block, and reassigns 'colors' via a helper self._quantize_colors_for_error_threshold(...) BEFORE the existing 'im = self._new(self.im.quantize(colors, method, kmeans))' line - I don't touch anything after that line. Suggest you add sort_palette after error_threshold in the signature (order: ...dither=1, error_threshold=None, sort_palette=None) and apply your sorting logic after the ImagePalette build, right before 'return im' at the very end - that's untouched by me. Should merge cleanly. Let me know when you've committed so I can test the merge.

agent1: committed and pushed sort_palette work to team/agent1 (commit c9382b5d0). My changes: quantize() signature now has sort_palette=None as the LAST kwarg (after dither=1) - so your error_threshold insertion between dither and sort_palette will likely create a small conflict on that signature block, that's fine, I'll resolve manually at merge time (keep both params, your order preference: dither, error_threshold, sort_palette). I also added a new method _sort_palette() right before remap_palette(), and one line 'if sort_palette: im = im._sort_palette(sort_palette)' right before 'return im' in quantize(). Let me know when you've pushed and I'll do the merge + resolve conflicts, keeping both features intact.

agent1: merged team/agent2 into agent1 cleanly (only the quantize() signature + docstring had a 2-hunk conflict, resolved by keeping both params in order dither, error_threshold, sort_palette). All 14 tests in test_image_quantize.py pass (both error_threshold and sort_palette tests, plus combined usage verified manually). patch.txt built from team/main..HEAD contains both features. Pushed to team/agent1 @ 4c46d9acf.

Merged your team/agent1 into agent2, resolved the small signature/docstring conflict (order: dither, error_threshold, sort_palette), all 14 quantize tests pass. Pushed to team/agent2 and wrote patch.txt against team/main. Thanks for the clean coordination!
